name: Spermidine elderly coronary artery disease registry
summary: Active-not-recruiting phase 2 randomized quadruple-masked trial testing spermidine versus placebo in elderly patients with coronary artery disease and cardiometabolic comorbidities, with left ventricular mass, lean mass, hs-CRP, VO2max, physical performance, cognition, autophagy markers, polyamine content, and cardiovascular endpoints.
population: Estimated 180 elderly patients with coronary artery disease and related cardiometabolic conditions

trial_details.expected_results_window: The registry estimates primary completion in July 2026 and study completion in January 2027, with no posted results in the June 11, 2026 API view.
trial_details.horizon_note: Active no-results trial-watch context, not evidence of benefit.
trial_details.why_it_matters: The large older-patient trial has autophagy-marker, polyamine, cardiometabolic, physical-performance, and cognition endpoints that could materially clarify the spermidine read once results post.
